questions.q: In which year was the Delhi Sultanate founded?
questions.explanation: The Delhi Sultanate is conventionally dated from 1206 CE, when Qutb-ud-din Aibak established Turkish rule at Delhi.

questions.q: In which year did the Delhi Sultanate effectively come to an end?
questions.explanation: The Sultanate ended in 1526 CE when Babur defeated Ibrahim Lodi at the First Battle of Panipat, founding the Mughal Empire.

questions.q: How many successive dynasties ruled the Delhi Sultanate?
questions.explanation: Five dynasties ruled in succession: the Mamluk (Slave), Khalji, Tughlaq, Sayyid, and Lodi.

questions.q: Which city served as the principal capital of the Delhi Sultanate?
questions.explanation: Delhi was the seat of the Sultanate, which takes its name from the city, though the capital briefly shifted under Muhammad bin Tughlaq.

questions.q: The Delhi Sultanate was a state ruled by sultans of which faith?
questions.explanation: The Delhi Sultanate was a Muslim state, its rulers being Turkish, Afghan, and other Muslim dynasts.

questions.q: Which empire supplanted the Delhi Sultanate in northern India?
questions.explanation: The Mughal Empire, founded by Babur in 1526, replaced the Delhi Sultanate as the dominant power in the north.

questions.q: The first dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate is commonly known by which name?
questions.explanation: The Mamluk or Slave dynasty, founded by the former slave Qutb-ud-din Aibak, was the first of the five dynasties.

questions.q: The Delhi Sultanate ruled during which broad period of Indian history?
questions.explanation: Spanning 1206 to 1526 CE, the Sultanate was a central feature of medieval Indian history.

questions.q: The last dynasty to rule the Delhi Sultanate was the:
questions.explanation: The Afghan Lodi dynasty was the fifth and final ruling house of the Sultanate before the Mughal conquest.

questions.q: Which battle of 1192 CE paved the way for the founding of the Delhi Sultanate?
questions.explanation: Muhammad of Ghor's victory over Prithviraj Chauhan at the Second Battle of Tarain in 1192 opened northern India to Turkish rule.

questions.q: Which is the correct chronological order of the five dynasties of the Delhi Sultanate?
questions.explanation: The dynasties ruled in the order Mamluk, Khalji, Tughlaq, Sayyid, and finally Lodi.

questions.q: The Khalji dynasty came to power in Delhi in which year?
questions.explanation: Jalaluddin Khalji founded the Khalji dynasty in 1290, ending the Mamluk line.

questions.q: The Tughlaq dynasty was established in Delhi in which year?
questions.explanation: Ghiyasuddin Tughlaq founded the Tughlaq dynasty in 1320 after the fall of the Khaljis.

questions.q: Which Sultan of Delhi notably attempted to shift the capital to Daulatabad in the Deccan?
questions.explanation: Muhammad bin Tughlaq's ambitious and ill-fated transfer of the capital to Daulatabad is a famous episode of Sultanate history.

questions.q: The Sayyid dynasty of Delhi came to power following whose devastating invasion of 1398?
questions.explanation: Timur's sack of Delhi in 1398 shattered Tughlaq power, and the Sayyids emerged in the weakened aftermath in 1414.

questions.q: Which Khalji Sultan introduced sweeping market price controls in Delhi?
questions.explanation: Alauddin Khalji (r. 1296-1316) is known for his rigorous market reforms and price regulations to sustain his army.

questions.q: Which Sultan founded the Lodi dynasty in 1451 CE?
questions.explanation: Bahlul Lodi founded the Afghan Lodi dynasty in 1451, ending the Sayyid line.

questions.q: Which Lodi Sultan is credited with founding the city of Agra as a capital?
questions.explanation: Sikandar Lodi founded Agra in 1504 and shifted his capital there from Delhi.

questions.q: Which religious tradition flourished alongside Sultanate rule, with figures like Nizamuddin Auliya?
questions.explanation: Sufi orders such as the Chishti, with saints like Nizamuddin Auliya, spread widely during the Sultanate period.

questions.q: The Persian poet and scholar associated with several Sultanate courts in Delhi was:
questions.explanation: Amir Khusrau, a poet and musician, served at the courts of several Delhi Sultans and is a celebrated figure of the era.

questions.q: Which Sultan repelled repeated Mongol invasions of the Sultanate's northwest frontier around 1300 CE?
questions.explanation: Alauddin Khalji successfully repulsed a series of Mongol incursions during his reign, strengthening the Sultanate's defences.

questions.q: The southern campaigns that extended Sultanate authority deep into the Deccan were led by generals of which Sultan?
questions.explanation: Alauddin Khalji's general Malik Kafur led campaigns that carried Sultanate power far into peninsular India.

questions.q: Which contemporary chronicler wrote the Tarikh-i-Firuz Shahi, a key history of the Sultanate?
questions.explanation: Ziauddin Barani's Tarikh-i-Firuz Shahi is a major source for the history of the later Mamluk, Khalji, and Tughlaq periods.

questions.q: The token currency experiment, issuing bronze and copper coins as legal tender, was undertaken by which Sultan?
questions.explanation: Muhammad bin Tughlaq's token currency, easily counterfeited, became a famous example of a failed monetary scheme.

questions.q: Which Sultan abolished many illegal taxes and is known for extensive public works including canals?
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah Tughlaq (r. 1351-1388) is remembered for canals, towns, and the founding of cities such as Firozabad.

questions.q: Under the Tughlaqs, the Bahmani Sultanate broke away in 1347 in which region?
questions.explanation: The Bahmani Sultanate's secession in the Deccan in 1347 marked the start of the Sultanate's loss of southern territories.

questions.q: Which last Mamluk-era Sultan strengthened royal authority and enforced strict court etiquette like the sijda?
questions.explanation: Balban restored the prestige of the crown, demanding prostration (sijda) and crushing rival nobles to centralise power.

questions.q: The iqta system, central to Sultanate administration, primarily concerned the:
options: Assignment of revenue from land in lieu of salary
Election of the Sultan by nobles
questions.explanation: Under the iqta system, officials and military commanders were granted revenue rights over territory in return for service.

questions.q: Which Sultan, the founder of the Tughlaq line, defeated the usurper Khusrau Khan to take the throne?
questions.explanation: Ghiyasuddin Tughlaq (Ghazi Malik) overthrew Khusrau Khan in 1320 to establish the Tughlaq dynasty.

questions.q: Which dynasty ruled for the longest single span among the five dynasties of the Delhi Sultanate?
questions.explanation: The Tughlaq dynasty (1320-1414) endured the longest of the five, despite later fragmentation of its empire.
